steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to contrast the locations of the two specimens. A successful answer must name both specimens, give both museum locations, and use language that signals a contrast between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Like Horridus, Dio is a Triceratops fossil specimen that lived in the Late Cretaceous period.': This emphasizes similarity (both lived in the same period), not a contrast in location. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada, houses Dio, a Triceratops fossil specimen.': This mentions only one specimen and one location — no contrast is possible with a single data point. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The Triceratops fossil specimen Horridus is not the only such specimen currently housed in a museum or institute.': Too vague — no locations are named and no contrast is drawn. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Horridus is housed at the Melbourne Museum in Melbourne, Australia, while Dio is housed at the Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada.': Names both specimens, gives both locations, and uses "while" to signal the contrast between Australia and Canada. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
